title: Full Professor (Tenured) in Environment and Society
description:
Asian School of the Environment

Nanyang Technological University, Singapore

Full Professor in Environment and Society


The Asian School of the Environment (ASE) (https://www.ntu.edu.sg/ase) is an interdisciplinary school at Nanyang Technological University (NTU) that uses Earth and environmental science, engineering, technology, and the social sciences to address key environmental challenges. Many ASE researchers work closely with the Earth Observatory of Singapore (https://earthobservatory.sg/), and/or Singapore Centre for Environmental Life Sciences Engineering (https://scelse.sg/), and/or Centre for Climate Change and Environmental Health, CCEH (https://www.ntu.edu.sg/cceh).


The Asian School of the Environment (ASE), College of Science (CoS) at the Nanyang Technological University (NTU) in Singapore seeks to appoint an accomplished and visionary scientist as Full Professor in Environment and Society. We welcome applicants with research interests including but not limited to environmental governance and decision-making, political ecology, environmental or ecological economics, risk and resilience in socio-environmental systems, and coupled human–environment systems. Candidates in relevant fields may also be considered for additional appointment at the Earth Observatory of Singapore (EOS).


Qualities and Leadership

Candidates should have a strong track record of impactful, interdisciplinary research, demonstrated leadership in securing major grants and building collaborative research networks, supervising graduate students, experience mentoring junior faculty, and contributions to institutional or programmatic development, especially within interdisciplinary environments. The successful candidate will have opportunities and will be expected to convene and inspire diverse teams, fostering collaborations across disciplines and institutions to develop, pursue, and lead large-scale, high-impact research initiatives that address pressing environmental and societal challenges.


Applicants must have a PhD in a relevant field and would normally already hold a tenured Professorial position in a university (or equivalent institution).


The successful candidate will:

• Build and lead a pioneering research programme in environment and society

• Convene and lead large collaborative projects and initiatives at regional and global scales

• Teach at undergraduate and postgraduate levels in ASE

• Contribute to mentoring and academic leadership within NTU

• Develop collaborations with researchers across NTU, Singapore, and internationally


ASE and CoS seeks a diverse and inclusive workforce and is committed to equality of opportunity. We welcome applications from all and recruit based on merit, regardless of age, race, gender, religion, marital status and family responsibilities, or disability.


Emoluments

The commencing salary is competitive and will be commensurate with the candidate's qualifications and experience. The University offers a comprehensive fringe benefit package.


Application Procedure

Interested applicants are invited to submit through the NTU career portal a completed application package which include:

1. Cover letter describing experience and interests in research, teaching, and leadership.

2. A full Curriculum vitae

3. Publication list

4. A summary of the future research, teaching and service plans, and

5. The names/emails of four individuals willing to provide letters of reference. References will only be contacted if you are shortlisted for the position.


All applications and materials submitted will be held in strict confidence.


The Search Committee will begin reviewing applications on 3 November 2025.


For further enquiries, please contact Search Committee Chair, Professor Steve Yim (steve.yim@ntu.edu.sg)


Please click here to apply.

postedCompany.description:
A research-intensive public university, Nanyang Technological University, Singapore (NTU Singapore) has 33,000 undergraduate and postgraduate students in the Engineering, Business, Science, Humanities, Arts, & Social Sciences, and Graduate colleges. It also has a medical school, the Lee Kong Chian School of Medicine, established jointly with Imperial College London.


NTU is also home to world-class autonomous institutes – the National Institute of Education, S Rajaratnam School of International Studies, Earth Observatory of Singapore, and Singapore Centre for Environmental Life Sciences Engineering – and various leading research centres such as the Nanyang Environment & Water Research Institute (NEWRI) and Energy Research Institute @ NTU (ERI@N).


Ranked amongst the world’s top universities by QS, NTU has also been named the world’s top young university for the past seven years. The University’s main campus is frequently listed among the Top 15 most beautiful university campuses in the world and has 57 Green Mark-certified (equivalent to LEED-certified) buildings, of which 95% are certified Green Mark Platinum. Apart from its main campus, NTU also has a campus in Novena, Singapore’s healthcare district.


Under the NTU Smart Campus vision, the University harnesses the power of digital technology and tech-enabled solutions to support better learning and living experiences, the discovery of new knowledge, and the sustainability of resources.


For more information, visit www.ntu.edu.sg
postedCompany.name: NANYANG TECHNOLOGICAL UNIVERSITY
